comment.body:
It’s still down by 8% today. And volatility isn’t up as Alma mentioned would happen yesterday. It’s just less down than what it would be. And 120 rip is 1.69% higher than where we closed yesterday. From a directional perspective, that’s a large rip.

Also, if we were shorting vol, then I guess you’d be up 8% today… or more.
So that’s good if you shorted puts. But why would you short puts if the whole narrative of that vol decay yesterday was attributed to the price of SPX staying below the JPM put strike at 5565. We are now trading well above it and held that support this morning.

So question for the thread, who made money yesterday shorting volatility, as Alma suggested?

Just an honest question. If there was a trade where you shorted vol as Alma suggested and you made money, would love to see it, even for educational purposes and to further cement the direct implementation of her analysis.

comment.body: With the preface that when I began reading Alma's material first on X and then after subscribing about 2 weeks ago, I felt like a true beginner (again).  And as someone who does not sell naked options, yesterday I thought about her post (vol down expected, supportive OpEx, her comment previously on bullish lean, that we needed a bump for more liquidity), and as importantly her educational posts, and with this in mind and using more typical trading strategy to find a setup/entry, I bought a call spread, the long leg close ATM and the short leg a further out OTM (PLTR 80/90). IV was of course juiced yesterday. The thinking was that the ATM leg has high gamma, if I get the directional move I'm expecting, then it goes ITM and as vol drops (as she signaled), the delta of that leg goes up, meanwhile the drop in vol has a suppressive effect on the OTM leg even with the move in spot.  And that's what happened.  Sold it this morning for solid profit.  I'd invite comment/input on any errors or flaws in this application of "selling vol", b/c as noted, I feel like I'm about at the level of my profile picture, a reasonably well trained dog.  But aspiring to reach child-like human proficiency soon :)

IV was of course juiced yesterday. The thinking was that the ATM leg has high gamma, if I get the directional move I'm expecting, then it goes ITM and as vol drops (as she signaled), the delta of that leg goes up, meanwhile the drop in vol has a suppressive effect on the OTM leg even with the move in spot.  And that's what happened.  Sold it this morning for solid profit.  I'd invite comment/input on any errors or flaws in this application of "selling vol", b/c as noted, I feel like I'm about at the level of my profile picture, a reasonably well trained dog.  But aspiring to reach child-like human proficiency soon :)
